item.title: Object Class
item.content:encoded:
I liked this one. Short to read and quite a funny/interesting concept (and I love the title).
I got just one question: If 1933 is humanoid and sentient, then why is it's Object Class Safe? I thought that all humanoid and/or sentient SCPs are per se classified as Euclid.

Still good and short SCP! +1

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image originates from here, licensed as NC-ND. As such, it is not compliant with CC 3.0 and has since been removed.

item.title: Crosslinks!
item.content:encoded:
It occurs to me that this feels like a wish gone horribly wrong. More horribly wrong than usual for wishes, I mean.

And this is a homeless guy.

Horribly fucked-up wishes, plus homelessness, plus literally constant intoxication, equals an encounter with SCP-1481.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As someone who makes their own Irish Cream, where does the veg oil come in? As far as I know (and how I've been making it for years), all you need is the heavy cream, whiskey, vanilla/almond extract, and condensed milk. I can see the sugar additive (I may have to try that, next time I make a batch), but oil?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i got it! i got it!

it starts spitting that shit and turns the acid into it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: alcoholic*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Ugh, I really don't like the idea of Santa-as-a-SCP, I'ma downvote this"

"Oh, wow, that was way better than expected. Upvote it is then."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And to think cream liquers were some of my favourite drinks - now all I can see is a Drunk Santa milking himself into a rake of bottles and glasses.

+1 from me. Sláinte! *dry retches*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The story behind this guy is just depressing and weird. Homeless guy dressed as Santa, breaking into people's homes, leaving people bottles of bodily fluids (even if those fluids are generally meant for consumption). I like that the author didn't take it in a humorous direction. Makes it even more sad.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A nice Christmas-themed SCP, because it managed to come off as more bizarre/scary than funny. +1

item.title: Re: Costume
item.content:encoded: Golden cage.

item.title: Costume
item.content:encoded:
Actually, why should the Foundation let him keep his Santa costume? A photo of him in what he was wearing at the time of capture, sure, but given his overall obstreperousness…

forcibly strip it off him. Then bathe and shave him. Then give him a standard humanoid uniform to wear. If he refuses to wear it, let him sit around naked in a cold cell.

Incinerate the Santa suit as a combined biohazard and fire hazard (he's been wearing it for literally years, it's soaked with his body fluids of which sweat is the least disgusting, and all of his body fluids are hugely flammable).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah right, I misunderstood it. Guess the wording is fine then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is incapable of digesting anything that is not a standard ingredient of Irish cream, including readily prepared Irish cream.

The thing is, I think he's NOT capable of ingesting readily prepared Irish Cream. He can only ingest its ingredients.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For my THIRD comment, I just want to say that the picture is perfect. That is a fucking Drunken Santa mugshot, no doubt about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be fair his entire body would fall apart at a cellular level if it contained no fluids but Irish cream. It's what makes him anomalous.

This substance adequately fulfills the functions of the fluids it replaces in SCP-1933's tissues, despite the fact that it renders normal biochemical processes essential to life impossible. Foundation scientists have been unable to determine how it manages this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A humorous article that makes drunken santa an SCP but still manages to be creepy and disgusting? Definitely deserving of an upvote.

This sentence is unclear though (and has a small spelling error):

It is incapable of digesting anything that it not a standard ingredient of Irish cream, including readily prepared Irish cream.

I think a better wording would be

It is incapable of digesting anything that is not readily prepared Irish cream or a standard ingredient of Irish cream.

Additionally, the names of SI units are usually written out in full when not attached to a measurement, i.e. millilitre instead of ml

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What in the world?

Two things: Wouldn't this be Euclid? (Sentient skips tend to be.) And why in the world would personnel be allowed to consume the guy's anomalous and potentially life-threatening fluids? That just seems like a bad temptation to put out there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is nitpicky, but:

If the vitreous humor in his eyeballs was replaced by Irish cream, the insides of his eyeballs would be opaque, rendering him blind. I guess you could say that since the vitreous humor is a gel it doesn't count as a fluid, and so isn't replaced. But if the intracellular fluids of the lens and cornea were replaced by Irish cream, those parts of the eyes would be opaque, rendering him blind anyways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …why would they? I mean, the Santa suit isn't doing anything to make him more difficult to contain or anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wouldn't the Foundation just take the Santa suit away from him? It's not like he goes into a rage state if you remove it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought it was okay.
It was few notches above "Meh" and a few below "Good".
No-voted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: D'awww. Kind-hearted drunk Santa. I was expecting a traditional angry-drunk Santa with something anomalous. But this is far better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. It's weird in a funny sort of way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe he's 2412-J's degenerate brother.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ahohoho, this is good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha! My uncle is now a scip. Huh. That's a sad joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 682's bodily fluids would transform into Coca Cola.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm on it; just let me figure out a way for it to go wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The entonation is pretty weird. I enjoy the idea, but the execution is a bit lackluster. E: changed my mind into -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, I never suggest cross testing, especially not with one of my least favorite skips, but I'd love to see an experiment log where they feed the juice to 682 to try to kill it. :-D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Christmas, Christmas, everyday is Christmas
When you drink a Santa Liqueur!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know about elsewhere, but here in the UK, Irish cream (or at the very least Baileys) is considered a classically Christmassy drink.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok, while I did enjoy this, I feel that I may be missing something. Is there some connection between Santa Clause and Irish cream, of which I am not aware? But regardless, this is still amusing enough to read to warrant an upvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ehh, I would like it better without the Santa pic. It would be better with hints of Christmas references without needing a picture being like ARE YOU FEELING THE CHRISTMAS NOW MR. KRABS

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, this is perfect! It hits just the right spot for Christmas. It starts as amusing and evolves into something sad and creepy.
One nitpick: ''large bottle'' is too vague; maybe state the volume.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I like this as a main lister a lot, actually. The breaking in to homes on Christmas really cemented it in creepy main list territory for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice job making a Santa's Helper. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know that there is no canon, but usually Level 0 personnel aren't permitted any contact with SCPs. Level 1 personnel would make more sense.

E: Aw, you did it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We already have SCP-2412-J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My last SCP was SCP-1325, the Easter frog. And I meant to say Level 0, not Class 0. My bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, and it made me laugh. I can't decide as to whether it'd be more fitting as a -J, though. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
May I ask you a question? What was your last SCP? And another one, what is "Class 0 personnel"? Did you mean Class-D1 personnel?

Footnotes
1. D-Class, in case Smapti sees this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. I don't know how I missed that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a rate module per te.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's been a long time since my last SCP, which incidentally, was also food-based and holiday-themed. Hopefully, it'll work just as well this time as it did then.

Merry Christmas, everyone!
